etymology.description: semantic 走(走) (zǒu, "walk") + phonetic 巳(巳) (sì) -> rise; lift; begin; get up
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 走(走) (zǒu, "walk") suggests movement or action, fitting with the meaning of rising or getting up. The phonetic component 巳(巳) (sì) was chosen for its sound, contributing to the pronunciation without directly affecting the meaning.

parts.english: sixth of the Twelve Earthly Branches

examples.chinese: 太阳升起(太陽升起)，天边染上橘色(天邊染上橘色)。
examples.pinyin: Tàiyáng shēng qǐ, tiānbiān rǎn shàng jú sè.
examples.english: The sun rises, painting the sky with orange.

examples.chinese: 我们要起床了(我們要起床了)。
新的一天开始了(新的一天開始了)，大家都充满活力(大家都充滿活力)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn de yī tiān kāishǐ le, dàjiā dōu chōngmǎn huólì.
examples.english: A new day begins, and everyone is full of energy.
